content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing the game Tree Destruction on his computer, and his goal is to destroy trees. A tree of size XXX is a connected, acyclic graph with XXX nodes.

The game works as follows:


Initially, Chef's score is 000.
Chef's goal is to remove all the nodes from the tree while maximizing his score.
At any time, Chef can remove any node with exactly one neighbour in the remaining tree.
By doing so, his score will increase by the distance between the removed node and the furthest node from it in the remaining tree.
Note that,


The distance is the number of edges on the shortest path between two nodes.
If there is a single node in the tree, we remove the node, and the score remains the same.
After playing this game for a while, Chef realized it was too easy. So he started playing the complex version of this game: Tree Destruction 2.0.

This version of the game is very similar to the one described above, except that this game has NNN levels numbered sequentially from 1 to NNN. Level YYY is the game Tree Destruction with a tree of YYY nodes. In addition, the levels have relationships with each other:


The tree of level YYY is the tree of level Y−1Y - 1Y−1 with just one extra edge connecting the node YYY to a node already in the tree of level Y−1Y - 1Y−1.

Note that a tree of level 111 consists of a single node(node 111, of course).
So this version of the game can be described using a list PPP of size N−1N - 1N−1, where PiP_iPi​ represents the node that i+1i + 1i+1 will be connected to form the level i+1i + 1i+1, for all 1≤i<N1 \leq i < N1≤i<N.

Chef needs help solving this new game version, so he's asked for your help.

For each of the NNN levels, you have to tell him the **maximum score** he can achieve.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a integer NNN — the number of levels in the game.
The second line of each test case contains N−1N - 1N−1 space-separated integers — the list PPP described above.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN space-separated integers — the **maximum score** that Chef can achieve in each level of the game.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

Level 111:

The optimal order of removal is the following: 111
So, the score is 000.


Level 222:

The optimal order of removal is the following: 111 222
So, the score is distance(1,2)=1distance(1, 2) = 1distance(1,2)=1.


Level 333:

The optimal order of removal is the following: 111 333 222
So, the score is distance(1,3)+distance(3,2)=2+1=3distance(1, 3) + distance(3, 2) = 2 + 1 = 3distance(1,3)+distance(3,2)=2+1=3.
